claim_bullets.bullet_text: Functions in JavaScript are values representing computations (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.30-38).
Functions are reference types, not value types (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.30-38).
Functions are applied to arguments to produce a value (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.30-38).
Functions can return values or evaluate expressions (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.30-38).
Functions can return other functions (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.30-38).

source_record_text: This chapter introduces basic functions in JavaScript, covering their representation as values, identity (as reference types), application, and how to return values and expressions from functions.
